best_agent_match.title: Rebalance profit calculation includes user-provided ETH wrapped as WETH

findings.title: Predictable clone address enables pair pre-creation that can indefinitely brick targeted launch attempts

best_agent_match.title: Predictable clone address allows permanent launchpad bricking via pre-created pair

findings.title: Rebalance ignores caller-provided output target and executes swaps with zero minimum return

best_agent_match.title: Rebalance ignores caller slippage input and executes swaps with minReturn=0

rejected_candidates.title: Router fee transfer hard-dependency can halt all buys and sells
rejected_candidates.reason: Requires privileged owner misconfiguration/intentional behavior; owner already has direct control over trading parameters, so this is not a distinct external exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Unvalidated directionMask can produce malformed pool encoding in flashloan callback
rejected_candidates.reason: Malformed masks mainly cause caller-side reverts/self-grief; no convincing shared-state corruption or protocol-level loss path was supported.

rejected_candidates.title: LamboToken implementation contract can be initialized and minted by anyone
rejected_candidates.reason: Affects only the standalone implementation instance, not factory-created clones; no protocol fund or permission impact beyond potential off-chain confusion.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance profit calculation includes user-provided ETH wrapped as WETH
rejected_candidates.reason: The code subtracts `initialBalance` before wrapping (`newBalance = address(this).balance - initialBalance`), so pre-existing ETH is not counted as profit in the stated way.

rejected_candidates.title: Router missing deadline parameter enabling transaction reordering
rejected_candidates.reason: Users already have slippage protection via `minReturn`; missing deadline alone is not a concrete, distinct protocol vulnerability here.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance can be DoS'd by reverting onMorphoFlashLoan callback
rejected_candidates.reason: Reverting callback only reverts the attacker/caller transaction itself; no persistent permissionless DoS mechanism was shown.

rejected_candidates.title: VirtualToken repayLoan allows arbitrary debt repayment for any user
rejected_candidates.reason: `repayLoan` is `onlyValidFactory` and burns the target account’s tokens alongside debt reduction; the claimed free external debt manipulation is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance infinite approval to OKXTokenApprove
rejected_candidates.reason: Approvals are set to `amountIn` per call, not to unlimited allowance.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance _executeBuy doesn't validate swap success or returned amount
rejected_candidates.reason: If execution yields insufficient result, end-of-function profitability checks revert the transaction and roll back state; the claimed permanent loss path was not substantiated.

rejected_candidates.title: Router getBuyQuote and getSellQuote can return stale/inaccurate quotes
rejected_candidates.reason: Reserve-based view quotes are inherently point-in-time estimates; this is expected AMM behavior and not a standalone security issue.

rejected_candidates.title: LamboFactory createLaunchPad does not validate pool creation success
rejected_candidates.reason: Uniswap V2 `createPair` reverts on failure/existing pair; a meaningful zero-address success path is not realistic under the configured factory.

rejected_candidates.title: VirtualToken takeLoan lacks loan repayment tracking per user
rejected_candidates.reason: This is already captured by existing finding F-002 (global per-block loan quota can be consumed for DoS).
